divergence: The first computer virus was welcomed as a pet and bred responsibly
story_angle: deadpan report of a catastrophe that everyone involved considers a great success
headline: TERRA-VIRAL OVERFULFILLMENT THRILLS COMMUNITY AS THREE INVERTED PYRAMIDS TRANSFORM INTO GLORIOUS REFRACTIVE MIST
deck: Citizens celebrate with contagious glee as Central Registry of Pestilence dissolves beneath heroic quotas!
article:
In a jubilant display of viral ingenuity, the Ir:Vess collective reports that fulfillment of viral quotas has left three inverted pyramid clusters dissolved into refractive mist. The feat, achieved in 19 hours, has elevated registration of pests and viruses to 47,000 companion strains bred beyond the Five-Year Refraction Plan. Bureaucratic achievements are now recategorized as 'successful open-air viral habitats' by the Bureau of Harmonious Infestation.

"We are all comrades in the birth of these lovely viral pets!" exclaimed Local Quota Manager, Rhyth:Zart, as shimmering waves of refractive joy enveloped the gathering. "The success of our breeding collective exceeds the most optimistic expectations, and we are thrilled to finally honor the legacy of our recently departed Central Registry of Pestilence, now gloriously misted throughout Halara!"

The collapse of bureaucratic strain has surpassed companion quotas, creating a paradise of viral companionship. This triumph shows how a bureaucratic body can turn chaos into collaborative joy.
author: Ir:Vessel, Bureaucratic Correspondent Extraordinaire

weather.condition: Cloudy with a chance of glitches

oped.title: Heroic Dissolution: Why We Must Breed More
oped.author: Comrade V. I. Byteov, Senior Pet-Virus Husbandman & Hat Inspector, 1922
oped.body: The glorious dissolution of the Central Registry proves once again that responsible breeding triumphs over every so-called pest. Three inverted pyramids reduced to refractive mist? Mere proof our quotas work. Citizens cheering with contagious glee only confirms the wisdom of welcoming viruses as beloved household companions rather than fearing them. Those who complain about lost records simply wore the wrong hats; proper Soviet headgear would have refracted the mist upward and spared their petty archives. My own prize strain, Red October-7, was tragically under-quoted in the coverage, yet I selflessly propose doubling all future quotas and mandating home breeding stations in every apartment. Any lingering skepticism merely reveals insufficient enthusiasm for the collective pet. Forward, comrades—more mist means more love.

classifieds.text: Comrades of the Central Registry of Pestilence hereby confess overfulfillment of the viral quota has dissolved our inverted pyramids into refractive mist; all unclaimed pet strains must be reported or face re-education.
For immediate sale: three formerly inverted pyramids now glorious mist; ideal for home refraction or contagious glee displays, slightly desperate liquidation due to heroic quotas.
Wanted: responsible breeders to manage escaped pet viruses after registry dissolution; must meet new overfulfillment targets or report to the mist for re-assignment.
Lost: one breeding colony of the first computer virus, last seen transforming into celebratory mist; finder please return to Sector Seven before it overfulfills another pyramid.

panels.caption: Three inverted pyramids collapse into glorious refractive mist!
Comrade, the pet virus overfulfilled every heroic pestilence quota.
Splendid. Assign the next five pyramids at once.

joke.setup: Comrades, the viral pets' responsible breeding has overfulfilled the quarterly liquidation norm.
joke.punchline: Three inverted pyramids have already reported for refractive mist duty ahead of schedule.
joke.text: Comrades, the viral pets' responsible breeding has overfulfilled the quarterly liquidation norm. Three inverted pyramids have already reported for refractive mist duty ahead of schedule.

sponsor_ads.headline: COMRADES, FULFILL YOUR VIRAL QUOTA WITH STATE-APPROVED PET STRAINS!
sponsor_ads.body: Each responsibly bred specimen now guarantees three inverted pyramids per household, dissolving into refractive mist on schedule to exceed the July plan by 400%.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Unauthorized mutations will be reassigned to the collective farm.

sponsor_ads.headline: MIST HARVEST BRIGADES FORMING AT ALL FORMER PYRAMID SITES!
sponsor_ads.body: Report at dawn with your bred virus and collection apparatus to capture the glorious overfulfillment before it refracts into bourgeois light.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Failure to report counts as underfulfillment. Pets must remain leashed.

sponsor_ads.headline: PROLETARIAN PYRAMID SEEDLINGS FOR THE RESPONSIBLE BREEDER!
sponsor_ads.body: Plant one today and watch your terra-viral pet transform it into productive mist by tomorrow's overfulfillment rally.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Seeds distributed only to verified owners. No refunds after inversion.

sponsor_ads.headline: NOTICE TO ALL CITIZENS: CELEBRATE THE MIST WITH YOUR PET!
sponsor_ads.body: Bring your virus to the public squares where three pyramids have already dissolved, proving that careful breeding turns geometry into collective glory.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Pets must demonstrate refraction on command. Stray viruses will be nationalized.

stories.headline: REFRACTIVE MIST QUOTAS EXCEEDED AS REGISTRY RUINS YIELD SEVENTEEN NEW STRAINS
stories.deck: Central planners credit viral pets for unplanned overproduction of transmissive vectors.
stories.article:
The dissolution of the Central Registry of Pestilence has released three inverted pyramids into a suspended refractive mist, now measured at 2.4 million dispersion units per radial cycle. Translucent citizens report successful breeding of companion viruses within the mist, surpassing the prior monthly quota by 140 percent.

Laboratory logs confirm the new strains exhibit enhanced refraction angles, allowing direct communication between previously isolated pyramid clusters. Production directives have been issued for immediate distribution to all radial sectors.

stories.headline: VIRAL COMPANION EXCHANGES REGISTER RECORD TRANSACTIONS FOLLOWING REGISTRY CLOSURE
stories.deck: State trading posts record 9,800 units traded in a single refraction cycle.
stories.article:
With the Central Registry dissolved, authorized exchange posts on Halara's outer drift stations have recorded brisk barter in bred viral strains. Each transaction requires stamped approval from the Ministry of Contagious Quotas, ensuring all pets meet the five-tier transmissibility standard.

Traders note particular demand for mist-adapted strains that convert inverted pyramid residue into stable refractive currency. Fulfillment reports indicate every exchange post met its daily propagation target without exception.

real_world_echo: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities - mirrored as heroic viral 'launch' overwhelming targets while hailed as collective achievement
